edges.description: Amyloidogenic ADan peptide is deposited in brain vessels and parenchyma.
edges.source: ITM2B frameshift generates the ADan amyloidogenic peptide

edges.description: The same ITM2B mutation also reduces availability of functional mature BRI2 at synapses.
edges.source: ITM2B frameshift generates the ADan amyloidogenic peptide

edges.description: Amyloid deposition is accompanied by severe downstream tau pathology and neurodegeneration.
Amyloidogenic ADan species can trigger neuronal-cell oxidative stress, mitochondrial cytochrome c release, mitochondrial membrane-potential loss, and caspase-dependent apoptosis in vitro.
Progressive tau-associated neurodegeneration contributes to dementia.
Widespread neurodegeneration contributes to the progressive ataxic phenotype.
Reduced mature synaptic BRI2 impairs excitatory neurotransmission.
BRI2 normally inhibits amyloid-beta precursor protein processing; reduced mature BRI2 in FDD models and human brain is associated with increased APP-derived metabolites including Abeta.
edges.target: Increased APP processing and Abeta burden

edges.description: Impaired excitatory neurotransmission likely contributes to the progressive cognitive decline of familial Danish dementia.
Increased APP processing provides Abeta that co-deposits with ADan in some vascular and parenchymal lesions.
edges.source: Increased APP processing and Abeta burden
ADan pE-challenged SH-SY5Y neuronal cell model

edges.description: Detection of ADan in CNS vessels and parenchyma reports the defining ADan amyloid deposition mechanism.

edges.target: ITM2B frameshift generates the ADan amyloidogenic peptide

evidence.reference_title: Familial Danish dementia: a novel form of cerebral amyloidosis associated with deposition of both amyloid-Dan and amyloid-beta
evidence.snippet: We showed that ADan is widely distributed in the central nervous system (CNS) in the leptomeninges, blood vessels, and parenchyma.

readouts.interpretation: Detection of ADan in CNS vessels and parenchyma reports the defining ADan amyloid deposition mechanism.

nodes.description: Differentiated human SH-SY5Y cells treated with pyroglutamate-modified ADan model ADan-driven oxidative stress, mitochondrial cytochrome c release, and caspase-mediated apoptosis.
nodes.id: ADan pE-challenged SH-SY5Y neuronal cell model

evidence.reference_title: Oxidative stress and mitochondria-mediated cell death mechanisms triggered by the familial Danish dementia ADan amyloid.
evidence.snippet: ADan pE challenge resulted, in addition to the release of cytochrome c to the cytoplasm, in a concurrent loss of mitochondrial membrane potential, indicated by the diffuse MitoTracker staining and poor co-localization of both signals (Figure 7B, bottom panel).

nodes.description: Pyroglutamate-modified ADan is neurotoxic in differentiated SH-SY5Y cells, inducing reactive oxygen species, cytochrome c release, mitochondrial membrane-potential loss, and caspase-3 activation.

evidence.reference_title: Oxidative stress and mitochondria-mediated cell death mechanisms triggered by the familial Danish dementia ADan amyloid.
evidence.snippet: ADan pE provoked DNA fragmentation after only 8 hour peptide challenge, with increased and statistically significant levels of nucleosome formation observed following 24h treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Oxidative stress and mitochondria-mediated cell death mechanisms triggered by the familial Danish dementia ADan amyloid.
evidence.snippet: ADan pE challenge resulted, in addition to the release of cytochrome c to the cytoplasm, in a concurrent loss of mitochondrial membrane potential, indicated by the diffuse MitoTracker staining and poor co-localization of both signals (Figure 7B, bottom panel).

evidence.reference_title: Oxidative stress and mitochondria-mediated cell death mechanisms triggered by the familial Danish dementia ADan amyloid.
evidence.snippet: In cells challenged with either ADan pE or ADan E, the ROS-scavenging ability of Trolox almost completely abolished caspase-3 activation, an indicator of its protective effect from ADan-induced toxicity.

experimental_models.description: Measures ADan-driven oxidative stress, mitochondrial dysfunction, and intrinsic apoptosis in a neuronal cell-line peptide-challenge model.
experimental_models.name: ADan pE-challenged SH-SY5Y neuronal cell model

nodes.description: Progressive ataxia reflects cerebellar and broader neurodegenerative involvement.

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD), also known as heredopathia ophthalmo-oto-encephalica, is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by cataracts, deafness, progressive ataxia, and dementia.

nodes.description: Audiology evaluation and hearing-support planning are relevant because hearing loss follows the ocular manifestations and may become severe by mid adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling familial British and Danish dementia.
evidence.snippet: Hearing impairment was reported to appear 10–20 years following the development of ocular problems.

nodes.description: Early cataracts are part of the classic familial Danish dementia syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD), also known as heredopathia ophthalmo-oto-encephalica, is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by cataracts, deafness, progressive ataxia, and dementia.

nodes.description: ADan accumulates in leptomeninges, vessel walls, and brain parenchyma, producing cerebral amyloid angiopathy and parenchymal deposits; Abeta can co-deposit in a subset of vascular and parenchymal lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Familial Danish dementia: a novel form of cerebral amyloidosis associated with deposition of both amyloid-Dan and amyloid-beta
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD) is pathologically characterized by widespread cerebral amyloid angiopathy (CAA), parenchymal protein deposits, and neurofibrillary degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Familial Danish dementia: a novel form of cerebral amyloidosis associated with deposition of both amyloid-Dan and amyloid-beta
evidence.snippet: Abeta was also present in a proportion of both vascular and parenchymal lesions.

nodes.description: Progressive cognitive decline is a defining late neurodegenerative manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD), also known as heredopathia ophthalmo-oto-encephalica, is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by cataracts, deafness, progressive ataxia, and dementia.

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: Molecular genetic analysis of the BRI gene in the Danish kindred showed a different defect, namely the presence of a 10-nt duplication (795-796insTTTAATTTGT) between codons 265 and 266, one codon before the normal stop codon 267.

nodes.description: A decamer duplication near the normal stop codon of ITM2B creates an extended precursor from which the amyloidogenic ADan peptide is released.
nodes.id: ITM2B frameshift generates the ADan amyloidogenic peptide

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: The decamer duplication mutation produces a frame-shift in the BRI sequence generating a larger-than-normal precursor protein, of which the amyloid subunit (designated ADan) comprises the last 34 C-terminal amino acids.

nodes.description: Loss of functional mature BRI2 at synapses impairs excitatory glutamatergic neurotransmission.

evidence.reference_title: Danish and British dementia ITM2b/BRI2 mutations reduce BRI2 protein stability and impair glutamatergic synaptic transmission.
evidence.snippet: Collectively, the data show that FDD and FBD mutations cause a reduction of BRI2 levels and function at synapses, which results in reduced glutamatergic transmission.

nodes.description: Loss of mature BRI2 function increases amyloid-beta precursor protein processing and Abeta metabolite burden in FDD model systems and a human FDD brain sample, providing a mechanistic bridge between ITM2B-related dementia and Alzheimer-like amyloid biology.
nodes.id: Increased APP processing and Abeta burden

evidence.reference_title: Increased AbetaPP processing in familial Danish dementia patients.
evidence.snippet: The levels of soluble Aβ42 were increased in FDD and AD brains compared to aging brains (FDD versus Con., 11-fold increase; AD1 versus Con., 4-fold increase; AD2 versus Con., 14-fold increase, Fig. 3A).

evidence.reference_title: Increased AbetaPP processing in familial Danish dementia patients.
evidence.snippet: Strikingly, AβPP haplodeficiency prevents memory and synaptic dysfunctions in FDDKI mice, supporting the claim that Danish dementia is mediated, like FAD, through toxic AβPP products [17].

nodes.description: Amyloid deposition is accompanied by severe downstream tau pathology and progressive neurodegeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Familial Danish dementia: a novel form of cerebral amyloidosis associated with deposition of both amyloid-Dan and amyloid-beta
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD) is pathologically characterized by widespread cerebral amyloid angiopathy (CAA), parenchymal protein deposits, and neurofibrillary degeneration.

nodes.description: Ophthalmology evaluation and cataract-directed care are clinically relevant because cataracts are an early, progressive feature of familial Danish dementia.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling familial British and Danish dementia.
evidence.snippet: Clinically, FDD is characterized by the development and progression of cataracts during the third decade of life (Strömgren et al. 1970).

nodes.description: Paranoid reactions may accompany late progressive dementia in familial Danish dementia.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling familial British and Danish dementia.
evidence.snippet: Progressive dementia probably begins in the sixth decade and may be associated with paranoid reactions and temporal disturbances of consciousness.

nodes.description: Progressive hearing loss is part of the core syndromic presentation.

evidence.reference_title: A decamer duplication in the 3' region of the BRI gene originates an amyloid peptide that is associated with dementia in a Danish kindred.
evidence.snippet: Familial Danish dementia (FDD), also known as heredopathia ophthalmo-oto-encephalica, is an autosomal dominant disorder characterized by cataracts, deafness, progressive ataxia, and dementia.

nodes.description: No disease-modifying treatment is established; care is supportive and may involve ophthalmology, audiology, neurology, rehabilitation, and dementia management matched to the staged manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling familial British and Danish dementia.
evidence.snippet: Clinically, FDD is characterized by the development and progression of cataracts during the third decade of life (Strömgren et al. 1970). Hearing impairment was reported to appear 10–20 years following the development of ocular problems. Severe to total perceptive hearing loss developed by the fifth decade of life, with vestibular reflex disturbances, slurred speech, and a swaying gait (Strömgren et al. 1970; Strömgren 1981).

nodes.description: Danish-dementia ITM2B mutations reduce mature BRI2 abundance at synapses.

evidence.reference_title: Danish and British dementia ITM2b/BRI2 mutations reduce BRI2 protein stability and impair glutamatergic synaptic transmission.
evidence.snippet: In vivo and in vitro studies show that both pathogenic mutations alter maturation of BRI2 resulting in reduced levels of functional mature BRI2 protein at synapses.
